title: Judicial Law Clerks
description: Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents.

tasks.text: Prepare briefs, legal memoranda, or statements of issues involved in cases, including appropriate suggestions or recommendations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research laws, court decisions, documents, opinions, briefs, or other information related to cases before the court.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Draft or proofread judicial opinions, decisions, or citations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with judges concerning legal questions, construction of documents, or granting of orders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review complaints, petitions, motions, or pleadings that have been filed to determine issues involved or basis for relief.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of changes in the law and inform judges when cases are affected by such changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attend court sessions to hear oral arguments or record necessary case information.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Review dockets of pending litigation to ensure adequate progress.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Communicate with counsel regarding case management or procedural requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to questions from judicial officers or court staff on general legal issues.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter information into computerized court calendar, filing, or case management systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Verify that all files, complaints, or other papers are available and in the proper order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate judges' meeting and appointment schedules.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in conferences or discussions between trial attorneys and judges.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare periodic reports on court proceedings, as required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise law students, volunteers, or other personnel assigned to the court.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain judges' law libraries by assembling or updating appropriate documents.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform courtroom duties, including calling calendars, administering oaths, and swearing in jury panels and witnesses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
